column4: two-card ordering interaction (Relentless Reaper vs {Psyche!}); fails transfer — no general rule beyond this specific pairing. Nearest 4.9, which doesn't own ordering between arbitrary effect pairs
!TENSION:no-effect-plays playable even though the stealth isn't needed

reaction usable at the moment {Form of Corruption} is put into play, protecting only against that card; fails transfer beyond this pairing. Nearest 2.1, which covers generic sequencing, not this abnormal-entry reaction window
confirms lock status alone doesn't bar targeting; fails non-obviousness — default targeting rules already establish this absent an explicit readiness restriction. Nearest 5.2
clarifies this card's political effect does not grant control of the opponent's minion; fails transfer — a negative scope statement about this card's own text. Nearest 6.2

cards entering play with a variable (X) counter count burn immediately if X=0; recurs verbatim at {Secret Horde}.1 so it transfers, but no section owns "counter-count-on-entry" mechanics — 1.10 owns burn triggers, 1.12 owns counters on hosts, neither claims the entry convention
card-specific usage condition (cannot use the burn option while controlling a ready Kiasyd); fails transfer — applies only to this card's own gating clause. Nearest 1.6
!TENSION:no-effect-plays a prohibition on additional strikes bars playing one even for a side benefit
confirms superior discipline doesn't change the card's stated prevention fraction; fails non-obviousness — card text controls and superior doesn't implicitly scale printed values. Nearest 4.5
describes this card's own targeting placement (played on the vampire about to take the damage); fails transfer — restates the card's own text. Nearest 4.5

!TENSION:unlock-effect-outlives-source this "does not unlock as normal" effect ends when its source card is burned/removed; contrast {Rowan Ring}.5/.6 below, where the equivalent effect survives the source's own burning
!TENSION:unlock-effect-outlives-source here the "does not unlock as normal" effect survives the Ring being burned — opposite of {Rötschreck}.10 above
!TENSION:unlock-effect-outlives-source same persistence-survives-source-removal claim, opposite of {Rötschreck}.10
burn blood effects are cumulative — nothing owns cumulative/stacking effects generally (watch-list gap, cf. {Betrayer}.3); 1.2 nearest as periodicity owner but doesn't claim stacking
confirms no default restriction against choosing the same target vampire multiple times across copies; fails non-obviousness — absent an explicit restriction this is already assumed. Nearest 3.1
pure two-card interaction (Sacrificial Lamb + {Soul Gem of Etrius} transfer-lock); fails transfer beyond this specific equipment pairing. Nearest 6.3
obvious application — an ability requiring a target resource (equipment to steal) cannot fire absent that resource; fails non-obviousness. Nearest 4.10

confirms this card can be played by minions of methuselahs not involved in the combat because its own text carries no participation restriction; fails transfer/non-obviousness — reads the absence of a restriction literally. Nearest 4.1

this card's per-play cap of one pool loss to a target regardless of vote count is the inverse of the cumulative/stacking watch-list gap (cf. {Betrayer}.3, {Sabbat Priest}.1 above) — "an effect capped once per card resolution, not per triggering instance" is a real transferable pattern with no owning section. Nearest 3.7.6 (votes) and 1.2 (stacking)
!TENSION:no-effect-plays dodge is permitted although it accomplishes nothing against this card
card-specific counter/discipline-bonus mechanics; fails transfer — no other card shares this exact counter-to-discipline mapping. Nearest 1.12
same gap as {Riddle Phantastique}.1 above — counter-count-on-entry burn convention has no owning section
an effect-created ally's "cost of zero" is a routine consequence of abnormal entry (not played from hand, so no cost was ever set); fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.6
